title: haplopappus validus subsp. torreyi
intro: Haplopappus validus subsp. torreyi, also known as Torrey's rabbitbrush, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its essential oils, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects. In herbal preparations, Torrey's rabbitbrush can be found in teas, salves, and infusions, which may be used to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and promote relaxation.
